Summarize the following passage in one or two sentences, preserving the main facts.

The city library will test extended weekend hours for six months beginning in October. Three neighborhood branches will stay open until 8 p.m. on Saturdays and will add Sunday service from noon to 5 p.m. A regional literacy grant will cover staffing and security, so the pilot will not reduce weekday programs. Officials will compare visitor counts, program attendance, and survey feedback with the same period last year before deciding whether to make the schedule permanent.
ground_truth: The library will use a literacy grant to pilot longer Saturday hours and new Sunday hours at three branches for six months starting in October. Officials will use visits, program attendance, and feedback to decide whether to keep the schedule.

prompt:
Summarize the following passage in one or two sentences, preserving the main facts.

Northstar Components has approved a 12 million dollar energy project at its main factory. Solar panels installed across four warehouse roofs are expected to provide about 40 percent of the site's annual electricity once they begin operating next spring. A battery system will be added by 2027 to store midday surplus for evening production. The company says construction will happen in phases without closing production lines or eliminating jobs, and it expects lower electricity purchases to repay the investment within nine years.
ground_truth: Northstar is investing 12 million dollars in rooftop solar expected to supply 40 percent of its factory electricity next spring, with battery storage planned by 2027. Installation will not halt production or cut jobs, and projected savings should repay the cost within nine years.

prompt:
Summarize the following passage in one or two sentences, preserving the main facts and limitations.

Researchers randomly assigned 120 adults with mildly elevated blood pressure either to their usual routines or to a walking program. Participants in the program were asked to walk briskly for 30 minutes on five days each week for 12 weeks. Their systolic blood pressure fell by an average of six points, compared with one point in the control group. The authors cautioned that the study was short, relied partly on self-reported exercise, and did not establish whether the improvement would persist for a year.
ground_truth: In a randomized 12-week study of 120 adults, brisk walking for 30 minutes five days a week reduced systolic blood pressure by about six points versus one in controls. The short duration and partly self-reported exercise leave long-term effects uncertain.

prompt:
Summarize the following notice in one or two sentences, preserving dates and practical impacts.

Because inspectors found structural damage on Mill Creek Bridge, school buses cannot use the usual crossing while repairs are underway. Beginning September 3, routes 12 and 18 will detour along Harbor Road. The Pine Street and Market Square stops will move temporarily to Oak Avenue, and morning pickups may occur up to 15 minutes earlier. Families will receive exact times through the district app. The change is expected to last eight weeks, although the district will issue an update if bridge work finishes sooner.
ground_truth: Starting September 3, bridge repairs will reroute school buses 12 and 18 via Harbor Road for about eight weeks, moving two stops to Oak Avenue and potentially making pickups 15 minutes earlier. Exact times and updates will be sent through the district app.

prompt:
Summarize the following passage in one or two sentences, preserving the measured result and unresolved issue.

A coastal restoration partnership has completed work on 45 hectares of marsh that had been choked by invasive reeds. Crews removed the reeds, reopened two tidal channels, and planted native cordgrass grown by a nearby university. Surveys this spring counted 28 percent more nesting shorebirds than before work began, and water tests showed improved oxygen levels. Researchers say at least three more years of monitoring are needed, however, and the project has not yet determined whether the restored marsh will reduce flooding during major storms.
ground_truth: Restoration of 45 hectares of marsh removed invasive reeds, reopened tidal channels, and replanted natives; early surveys found 28 percent more nesting shorebirds and better oxygen levels. Longer monitoring is needed, and any flood-protection benefit remains unknown.

prompt:
Summarize the following incident report in one or two sentences, preserving what was and was not exposed.

A payroll vendor discovered that an employee entered credentials into a convincing phishing page, allowing an attacker to access one customer-support database for roughly two hours. The exposed records contained names and email addresses for about 8,400 people. The company says the database did not contain passwords, bank details, payment-card numbers, or government identification. It disabled the compromised account, reset staff credentials, added a second approval step for remote access, and began notifying affected customers on Friday.
ground_truth: A phishing attack exposed names and email addresses for about 8,400 people during two hours of database access, but not passwords, financial data, or government IDs. The vendor disabled the account, reset credentials, strengthened remote access, and is notifying customers.

prompt:
Summarize the following passage in one or two sentences, preserving the timeline and quantities.

Divers surveying a merchant shipwreck in 2019 recovered 214 silver coins fused together by corrosion. Conservators spent four years separating and stabilizing the pieces with low-current electrolysis, revealing dates that place the cargo in the early eighteenth century. A new museum exhibition opening in November will display 70 of the best-preserved coins alongside navigation tools from the wreck. The remaining coins will stay in climate-controlled storage so researchers can study trade routes and metal sources without exposing the fragile surfaces to repeated handling.
ground_truth: After four years of conservation, 214 silver coins recovered from a 2019 shipwreck were identified as early eighteenth century. Seventy will go on display in November, while the rest remain stored for research.

prompt:
Summarize the following passage in one or two sentences, preserving the measured benefits, financing, and concern.

A farming cooperative replaced flood irrigation with drip lines across 300 hectares of tomato fields. Meter readings from the first full season show water use fell 32 percent while average tomato yield rose 9 percent. The equipment's upfront cost was covered by a five-year low-interest loan shared among 46 farms, rather than by a government grant. Agronomists welcomed the results but warned that reduced flushing could allow salts to accumulate in some soils, so the cooperative will add salinity testing before the next planting season.
ground_truth: Drip irrigation across 300 hectares cut water use 32 percent and raised tomato yields 9 percent, financed by a five-year loan shared by 46 farms. The cooperative will monitor possible soil-salt buildup before next season.

prompt:
Summarize the following project update in one or two sentences, preserving the cause, schedule, cost, and current service.

The Eastlink rail extension will open in late 2028 rather than 2027 after tunneling crews encountered fractured rock and unexpected groundwater beneath the river. Engineers must redesign part of the lining and slow excavation while new pumps are installed. The changes are projected to add 430 million euros to the budget. Existing trains on the central line will continue operating during construction, and no stations are scheduled to close. The transport authority has commissioned an independent review of the geological surveys and will publish its findings next spring.
ground_truth: Unexpected fractured rock and groundwater have delayed the Eastlink extension from 2027 to late 2028 and added an estimated 430 million euros. Existing central-line service will continue, while an independent review examines the earlier geological surveys.
